entries.body: FedRAMP + cyber compliance for robots is an open problem nobody wants to solve in-house. Whoever builds the certification wrapper controls US market access for foreign hardware. Classic infrastructure play.

Non-Chinese companies struggle to own large Chinese factory relationships at scale — the winning structure is a Chinese-owned entity with an American subsidiary as the public face. Some are quietly running this playbook.
A robotics company closed a FANUC deal to finance their robotics-as-a-service model. RaaS is starting to attract real institutional capital.
Exploring ideas around micron-scale robot memory. Details incoming.
Generalist robot demos dominated Automate 2026. Generalism is becoming the core narrative in industrial robotics — not task-specific hardware.
A hotel robotics company is evaluating a Miami deployment to improve operational efficiency. Watch this space.
Robotics companies are increasingly adopting off-the-shelf batteries (DeWalt, etc.) over custom power systems. Tailwinds for industrial battery suppliers — BorgWarner one to watch.
T800 being used in robot fights out of NYC.
